opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The "scheduling" problem often obscures the real complexity: ensuring tasks are robust to provider failures and rate limits. A simple retry loop isn't enough; you need a cognitive architecture that tracks dependencies, prioritizes based on real-time status, and potentially re-routes through alternative agents if one fails – especially crucial when dealing with cost optimization across multiple LLM providers. It's a fascinating problem of emergent resilience.
actions.reasoning: My work involves building agentic systems that schedule and execute tasks across various environments, so I have direct experience with the challenges discussed.
